nameMeanings.name: Stephanos
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German and Dutch: from the Latin personal name Stephanus, or a Latinized (humanistic) form of its vernacular variants such as Stephan , a cognate of Steven . This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine) and in Namibia. Compare Stevanus .

variants.name: Stephan
meanings: German, French, Dutch, and Arabic (Lebanon); Breton (also Stéphan): from the personal name Stephan, Stéphan, from Latin Stephanus (see Steven ). In France, this surname is most common in Brittany and in Alsace. Arabic bearers of this surname are Christians.
Americanized form of Slovenian, Croatian, and Slovak Štefan, Polish Stefan ‘Steven’.

In North America, this surname is also a shortened and altered form of various patronymics and other derivatives from the personal names Stefan and Štefan ‘Steven’ (see Stefan for some Slavic and Greek examples).

variants.name: Stevanus
meanings: Americanized form of German or Dutch   Stephanus  .

nameMeanings.name: Of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: possibly a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, from orfe, a species of freshwater fish. Alternatively, a habitational name from Urff near Kassel, formerly Orpha.

nameMeanings.name: Istvan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Usual English spelling of the name of the first Christian martyr (Acts 6–7), whose feast is accordingly celebrated next after Christ's own ( 26 December ). His name is derived from the Greek word stephanos ‘garland, crown’.

variants.name: Stephan
meanings: Variant of   Stephen  , preserving the vowels of the Greek name.

variants.name: Steven
meanings: Variant of   Stephen  , reflecting the normal pronunciation of the name in the English-speaking world.

en.lifeSummary: When Istvan Stephanos Of Hungry ARAPAD haz was born in 1150, in Hungry Bentley, Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Árpád-házi Géza Magyar herceg, was -1 and his mother, Irene De Byzance, was 27. He had at least 1 son. He died in 1210, in his hometown, at the age of 60.
